rag_rules: Place AGREE assumptions and guarantees in component type annexes when they refer to visible ports, and in implementation annexes for subcomponent bindings.
Write guarantees that relate visible outputs to visible inputs, avoiding implementation-only subcomponent paths in type contracts.
generic_rules: Use const and eq declarations with valid AGREE scalar types: int, real, bool.
Ensure type consistency between assumed types and actual AADL data types in expressions.
Only include assumptions and guarantees in AGREE annexes when concrete bounds or visible relations are provided.
Avoid generating clauses that compare Boolean domain values to integers unless the port is explicitly an AGREE bool.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Component type contracts should reference component ports directly, not subcomponent ports unless represented through variables.
Use exact Boolean expressions for Boolean ports; avoid numeric comparisons on opaque data.
anti_patterns: Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type annexes.
Do not generate range assumptions or guarantees without concrete bounds or visible relations.
irrelevant_topics: Model fusion and owner scope rules
